title: Harmonia
description: First village entry. Introduce the village, the party, the mood. No quest yet — just arrival.

lines: Harmonia was the kind of village that existed to be left. A waypoint. A place you were from, not a place you stayed.
Six houses, a chapel, a shop that sold swords it had never tested, and an inn whose slime mascot had been repainted so many times it now resembled a vaguely threatening cloud.
The monsters were getting worse. The bats were bigger. The slimes were angrier. Traders stopped coming.

steps.text: So four people showed up. Not because they were brave. Not because they were special. Because they were there, and nobody else was.

lines.text: The Fighter came because he had heard there was trouble, and trouble was what fighters were for.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: If there's trouble, I'll stand in front of it. That's... what I do.
The Cleric came because the chapel had been empty for a week and God, or whatever heard her prayers, had clearly decided someone should investigate.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: A calling is a calling. You don't second-guess it.
The Mage came because the pattern he'd found in the monster attacks was too interesting to ignore.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The attack frequency follows a geometric progression. Someone is designing these encounters.
The Rogue came because Elder Theron had seen a figure sleeping under the inn's eaves and pointed.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: I was passing through. Made the critical error of still being here.
And the Bard came because the Bard went everywhere there was a story.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: What a tale this shall be!

steps.text: A notice had been nailed to the door of the Sleepy Slime Inn. It said to report to Elder Theron at the village square.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Elder Theron. Let's find him — he's supposed to be somewhere in the village.
